item.title: Will a new central air conditioning unit lower your electric bill?
item.description: A new AC system may reduce your energy costs, but the savings depend on more than just the equipment you install.

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenges to AR-15 bans
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to take up challenges to so-called assault-weapons bans in Cook County, Illinois, and Connecticut.

item.title: U.S.-Iran peace efforts inch along, mired by Israel's fight with Hezbollah
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: Venezuela hits crucial point for rescues after earthquakes
item.description: The search for missing Venezuelans is hitting a critical point after last week's deadly earthquakes. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: Colorado voters head to polls for key primaries including governors' race
item.description: Colorado voters are headed to the polls on Tuesday for key primaries, including the gubernatorial race. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.

item.title: Maps show heat dome forecast to scorch major U.S. cities this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast much of the eastern U.S. this week, and forecasters say temperatures will feel even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: Brandon Lake opens up about his music and why he thinks his songs are "open doors"
item.description: Grammy-winner Brandon Lake is one of the biggest names in Christian music. He speaks with "CBS Mornings" about how his audience is growing, collaborations and how he got his start.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds state transgender athlete bans in girls' and women's sports
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld state laws from West Virginia and Idaho that restricted participation by transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports.

item.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean returns to Congress, says he was diagnosed with depression
item.description: The New Jersey congressman missed more than 140 votes since March 5 as those around him declined to give specifics about his medical issue.

item.title: Rep. Tom Kean of New Jersey returns to Congress, explains he was being treated for depression
item.description: After a monthslong absence for medical reasons, Rep. Tom Kean, a Republican from New Jersey, returned to Congress on Tuesday, June 30, and said he had been receiving treatment for depression. Watch his full remarks on the House floor.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, striking down Trump's order
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down President Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship.

item.title: Supreme Court rules against Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down President Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship. Tony Dokoupil anchored this CBS News special report.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down coordinated campaign spending limits
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down federal limits on the amount of money a political committee can spend in coordination with federal candidates.

item.title: Arrests made after bomb attack targets reporter threatened by mafia
item.description: Sigfrido Ranucci had just returned home at the time of the explosion, and his daughter had walked by a half-hour before, his employer said.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down federal limits on coordinated campaign spending
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down coordinated campaign spending caps on Tuesday in a major move ahead of the 2026 midterm election. CBS News contributor Rebecca Roiphe has more.

item.title: Federal student loan changes become effective this week. Here's who may be impacted.
item.description: New repayment rules and borrowing limits start this week. Here's who should pay close attention to the changes.

item.title: Woman killed by alligator while swimming in Florida river
item.description: Officials say people and pets should keep a safe distance away from the water to limit the chances of an alligator encounter.

item.title: Supreme Court allows state bans on transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld state bans prohibiting transgender athletes from competing in girls' and women's sports. Tony Dokoupil anchors this CBS News Special Report.

item.title: Trump side swipes bipartisan housing bill and demands SAVE America Act passing
item.description: President Trump told reporters Tuesday in the Oval Office that the bipartisan housing affordability bill he was set to sign last week was "a big yawn" compared to the SAVE America Act. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: Trump's envoys in Qatar for talks on Iran as tensions remain sky high
item.description: U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are in Doha, Qatar, for talks on Iran amid mixed messaging from both sides. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean has the latest.

item.title: Emergency doctor's tips to survive extreme heat that will broil entire cities
item.description: Waves of extreme heat will broil cities across the U.S. this week. Hydration is key to staying safe during extreme heat, says Dr. Reed Caldwell, a doctor with the NYU Langone Health's Ronald O. Perelman Center for Emergency Services.

item.title: AI could make people dull, one scientist fears. Here's why.
item.description: AI tends to "play it safe within a user's preferences," nudging people toward more conventional choices, according to computational social scientist Sandra Matz.

item.title: Democratic primaries in Colorado mark latest fight over party's direction
item.description: One week after Democratic insurgent victories in New York, the focus is now moving to Colorado, where challenges in a trio of races are threatening candidates backed by the party establishment.

item.title: Fossil found in drawer turns out to be first dinosaur bone from Antarctica
item.description: Dinosaur fossils are rare to find in Antarctica because of the unforgiving ice caps. But millions of years ago, the region was populated by lush forests.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: Here's what to know about Medicare's new GLP-1 weight-loss drug program
item.description: For the first time, Medicare will cover GLP-1 drugs prescribed solely for weight loss for eligible beneficiaries at a $50 monthly copay.

item.title: Iran war has cost Americans $1,000 per household, economist estimates
item.description: The Iran war has significantly driven up the cost of fuel, airfare and other U.S. goods, according to Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi.

item.title: Aide who planned JFK Jr.'s secret wedding weighs in on Swift-Kelce rumors
item.description: RoseMarie Terenzio, who was JFK Jr.'s former chief of staff and planned his secret wedding to Carolyn Bessette, said she doesn't think Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are getting married at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Senate Ethics Committee dismisses misconduct complaint against Gallego
item.description: The bipartisan committee said it "did not find evidence that your actions violated federal law, Senate rules or related standards of conduct."
